description: Issue #183: CIDFont / CMap encoding support for multilingual PDFs

userStories.title: Implement ToUnicode CMap parser
userStories.description: Parse /ToUnicode CMap streams embedded in PDF font dictionaries. These provide direct CID → Unicode mappings using beginbfchar/endbfchar and beginbfrange/endbfrange operators. This is the highest-value CMap feature as many CIDFont PDFs include ToUnicode maps.
acceptanceCriteria: ToUnicode CMap streams are parsed correctly (bfchar and bfrange entries)
At least 3 previously-failing CIDFont PDFs now extract chars > 50%
Handles multi-byte Unicode mappings (e.g., surrogate pairs, BMP and SMP)
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (font setup). ToUnicode CMap format: 'beginbfchar <src> <dst> endbfchar' and 'beginbfrange <srcLo> <srcHi> <dst> endbfrange'. The CMap is a stream object referenced by /ToUnicode in the font dictionary. Many PDFs that currently score 0% include ToUnicode maps that would enable extraction if parsed.

userStories.title: Implement predefined CMap support for CJK fonts
userStories.description: CIDFont dictionaries reference predefined CMaps by name (e.g., 'Adobe-Japan1-UCS2', 'GBKp-EUC-H', 'UniKS-UCS2-H'). These standard CMaps define CID → character code mappings for CJK languages. Bundle the most common predefined CMaps or implement a CMap file loader.
acceptanceCriteria: At least Adobe-Japan1, Adobe-GB1, Adobe-Korea1, Adobe-CNS1 CMap families are supported
Japanese PDFs (issue3521, issue7696, issue8570, issue9262) extract chars > 30%
userStories.notes: Predefined CMaps can be bundled as static data or loaded from files. The Adobe CMap resources are open source (https://github.com/niceferrari/adobe-cmap). Key CMaps to support: UniJIS-UCS2-H, UniGB-UCS2-H, UniKS-UCS2-H, UniCNS-UCS2-H, Identity-H, Identity-V. Consider using a build script to embed CMap data.

userStories.title: Implement CIDFont width handling (W/DW arrays)
userStories.description: CIDFont dictionaries specify glyph widths via /W (width array) and /DW (default width) entries. The /W array format is: [cid [w1 w2 ...]] or [cidFirst cidLast w]. Implement width extraction from these arrays for correct character positioning.
acceptanceCriteria: CIDFont /W and /DW entries are parsed and used for char width calculation
CIDFont PDFs have improved x-coordinate accuracy
No regression on Type1/TrueType font widths
userStories.notes: Key file: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (font metrics setup). The /W array has a complex format: it's an array of entries where each entry is either [cid [w1 w2 ...]] (consecutive widths starting at cid) or [cidFirst cidLast w] (same width for range). /DW is the default width (typically 1000). These widths are in 1/1000 of the font's em-square.
